explanation: Utilising the energy from ATP hydrolysis, the myosin head binds to the exposed active sites on actin to form a cross bridge. (VI) Sarcomere is the functional unit of relaxation.
explanation: I, II, IV, V are correct. The incorrect statement can be corrected as Sarcomere is functional unit of contraction in skeletal muscle. In the centre of each 'I' band is an elastic fibre called ' Z ' line which bisects it.

Assertion: Eukaryotic cells have the ability to adopt a variety of shapes and carry out directed movements


Reason: There are three principal types of protein filaments i.e. actin filament, microtubules and intermediate filaments which constitute the cytoskeleton.
explanation: Eukaryotic cells (particularly animal and lower plant cells) have the ability to adopt a variety of shapes and carry out directed movements because they have a network of protein fibres that support the shape of the cell and anchor organelles to a fixed location. This network is called cytoskeleton. The cytoskeleton is made up of three types of protein filaments: actin filaments, microtubules and intermediate filaments.
